summary: Clean implementation of content-type typography defaults and thorough mission finalization. TypographyConfig struct is well-designed with clear separation of concerns. ContentType enum extension (Ui, Unknown) follows Rust idioms. Exhaustive match ensures Unknown falls back to prose defaults. The research document update is comprehensive and honest about deviations. 17 tests cover all validation assertions including end-to-end perceptual rendering verification.

observations: TypographyConfig is a clean value type with public fields — good for a configuration struct
Features stored as parseable strings rather than pre-parsed Feature structs — sensible since parsing happens at shaping time
ContentType enum variants are exhaustively matched everywhere (no wildcards)
typography.rs is a focused module (~110 lines) with clear responsibility boundaries
Research document updates are honest about deviations from the original plan
FallbackChain correctly handles new Ui and Unknown variants using pattern matching
Code defaults (+calt, +tnum) match the spec requirement for programming ligatures and tabular figures
Prose defaults (+onum) correctly enables oldstyle figures
UI defaults (weight_preference=500) correctly targets medium weight
Unknown → prose_defaults() ensures no panic on unrecognized content types
optical_sizing flag correctly set: true for prose (where opsz makes sense), false for code/UI
The pipeline integration test (code vs prose different glyph output) validates end-to-end wiring
VAL-CTYPE-001: Code → monospace + calt + tnum — verified with 3 tests
VAL-CTYPE-002: Prose → proportional + onum + optical sizing — verified with 3 tests
VAL-CTYPE-003: UI → proportional + medium weight + tracking — verified with 3 tests
VAL-CTYPE-004: Pipeline wiring with different shaped output — verified with 2 tests
VAL-CTYPE-005: Unknown → sane defaults matching prose — verified with 3 tests
VAL-CROSS-003: Auto-opsz, auto-weight-correction, and combined — verified with 3 tests
VAL-CROSS-004: Research document update — verified via grep for completion markers
Configuration module with no external input, no security concerns
